article_title: B.C. Reg. 30/2026 – British Columbia Securities Commission

minutes_briefs.title: Investment dealers must now register with the Canadian Investment Regulatory Organization (CIRO)
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC Securities Commission has replaced the old rule that let the Investment Industry Regulatory Organization of Canada (IIROC) handle registrations. Now, the Canadian Investment Regulatory Organization (CIRO) is the only group authorized to process registrations for investment dealers and mutual fund dealers in BC. This means if you are an investment dealer or mutual fund dealer, you must submit all new applications, renewals, reinstatements, and changes to CIRO instead of the old regulator. CIRO can also require you to post a bond (a type of insurance deposit).
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you are an investment dealer or mutual fund dealer, update your registration process to submit all applications to the Canadian Investment Regulatory Organization (CIRO).
minutes_briefs.source_body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 30/2026
Made under: Securities Act
Published in: No. 5 – B.C. Regs. 29 to 42, March 24, 2026
Deposit date: March 11, 2026

B.C. Reg. 30/2026, deposited March 11, 2026, under theSECURITIES ACT[section 184]. Rule of the British Columbia Securities Commission, dated March 10, 2026. The British Columbia Securities Commission orders that (a)	BC Instrument 22‑502Registration by the Investment Industry Regulatory Organization of Canada, B.C. Reg. 107/2008, is repealed, and (b)	BC Instrument 22‑502Registration by the Canadian Investment Regulatory Organization, as set out in the attached schedule, is made. — B. LEONG,British Columbia Securities Commission. BC INSTRUMENT 22‑502REGISTRATION BY THE CANADIAN INVESTMENT REGULATORYORGANIZATION Authorization to exercise powers and perform duties 1(1)	The Canadian Investment Regulatory Organization is authorized to exercise the powers and perform the duties under Part 5 of theSecurities Actwith respect to an applicant for registration or a person registered as (c)	a dealing representative of (d)	a chief compliance officer of (e)	an ultimate designated person of (2)	An application for registration, or an application for renewal or reinstatement of registration or an amendment to registration, must be submitted to the Canadian Investment Regulatory Organization. (3)	The Canadian Investment Regulatory Organization is authorized to require an investment dealer or a mutual fund dealer to deliver a bond. 2(1)	The Canadian Investment Regulatory Organization may delegate the exercise of a power or the performance of a duty referred to in this rule to a committee comprised of employees or directors of the Canadian Investment Regulatory Organization. (2)	For certainty, a decision of a committee referred to in subsection (1) is a decision of the Canadian Investment Regulatory Organization. Notification or submission of information "permitted individual"has the same meaning as in National Instrument 33‑109Registration Information; "person or company"means a person or company as defined in National Instrument 14‑101Definitions. (2)	For the purposes of Parts 3 and 4 of National Instrument 33‑109Registration Information, if a person or company listed below is required under that Instrument to give notice or submit information to the regulator, the notification or submission must be made to the Canadian Investment Regulatory Organization: (a)	an investment dealer giving notice or submitting information under section 4.1 of National Instrument 33‑109Registration Informationin respect of a permitted individual;
